title: PathPatternFeatures Object
description: Collection that provides access to all of the existing path pattern features in a component and supports the ability to create new path pattern features.

methods.name: add
methods.description: Creates a new path pattern feature.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: createInput
methods.description: Creates a PathPatternFeatureInput object. Use properties and methods on this object to define the path pattern you want to create and then use the Add method, passing in the PathPatternFeatureInput object.

methods.name: item
methods.description: Function that returns the specified path pattern feature using an index into the collection.

methods.name: itemByName
methods.description: Function that returns the specified path pattern feature using the name of the feature.

properties.name: count
properties.description: The number of path pattern features in the collection.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

related_samples.name: Path Pattern Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new path pattern feature.

add.title: PathPatternFeatures.add Method
add.description: Creates a new path pattern feature.
add.return_description: Returns the newly created PathPatternFeature object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: input
parameters.description: A PathPatternFeatureInput object that defines the desired path pattern. Use the createInput method to create a new PathPatternFeatureInput object and then use methods on it (the PathPatternFeatureInput object) to define the path pattern.

related_samples.name: pathPatternFeatures.add
related_samples.description: Demonstrates the pathPatternFeatures.add method using a selected body and sketch curve as the path.

classType.title: PathPatternFeatures.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

createInput.title: PathPatternFeatures.createInput Method
createInput.description: Creates a PathPatternFeatureInput object. Use properties and methods on this object to define the path pattern you want to create and then use the Add method, passing in the PathPatternFeatureInput object.
createInput.return_description: Returns the newly created PathPatternFeatureInput object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: inputEntities
parameters.description: The collection can contain faces, features, bodies or occurrences. All of the entities must be of a single type. For example, it can't contain features and occurrences but only features or occurrences.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: The Path object that represents a single set of connected curves along which to drive the pattern.

parameters.name: quantity
parameters.description: Specifies the number of instances in the first direction.

parameters.name: distance
parameters.description: Specifies the distance. How this value is used depends on the value of the PatternDistanceType property. A negative value can be used to change the direction. If the value is ExtentPatternDistanceType then it defines the total distance of the pattern. If the value is SpacingPatternDistanceType then it defines the distance between each element.

parameters.name: patternDistanceType
parameters.description: Specifies how the distance between elements is computed.

related_samples.name: pathPatternFeatures.add
related_samples.description: Demonstrates the pathPatternFeatures.add method using a selected body and sketch curve as the path.

item.title: PathPatternFeatures.item Method
item.description: Function that returns the specified path pattern feature using an index into the collection.
item.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if an invalid index was specified.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The index of the item within the collection to return. The first item in the collection has an index of 0.

itemByName.title: PathPatternFeatures.itemByName Method
itemByName.description: Function that returns the specified path pattern feature using the name of the feature.
itemByName.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if the specified name was not found.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the feature within the collection to return. This is the name seen in the timeline.

count.title: PathPatternFeatures.count Property
count.description: The number of path pattern features in the collection.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = pathPatternFeatures_var.count

isValid.title: PathPatternFeatures.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = pathPatternFeatures_var.isValid

objectType.title: PathPatternFeatures.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = pathPatternFeatures_var.objectType
